queries.name: Mass Subscriber Authentication Failure Detection
queries.pain_point: HSS database corruption or synchronization issues affecting subscriber authentication
queries.description: Detect mass authentication failures using statistical z-score analysis across 5-minute intervals, enriched with subscriber context to identify enterprise customer impact and HSS database issues before help desk is overwhelmed
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_failure_events | WHERE authentication_attempt == true AND failure_code IN ("AUTH_FAILURE", "HSS_UNREACHABLE", "SUBSCRIBER_UNKNOWN") | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(5 minutes, @timestamp) | STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_imsi = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi), unique_mme = COUNT_DISTINCT(mme_host) BY time_bucket | INLINESTATS avg_failures = AVG(failure_count), stddev_failures = STD_DEV(failure_count), avg_imsi = AVG(unique_imsi), stddev_imsi = STD_DEV(unique_imsi) | EVAL z_score_failures = (failure_count - avg_failures) / COALESCE(stddev_failures, 1), z_score_imsi = (unique_imsi - avg_imsi) / COALESCE(stddev_imsi, 1), imsi_change_rate = unique_imsi - avg_imsi | WHERE (z_score_failures > 3 OR z_score_imsi > 3) AND failure_count > 200 AND unique_imsi > 400 | EVAL severity = CASE(z_score_imsi > 4 AND unique_imsi > 500, "CRITICAL", z_score_imsi > 3 AND unique_imsi > 400, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP time_bucket, severity, unique_imsi, failure_count, z_score_imsi, unique_mme, imsi_change_rate | SORT z_score_imsi DESC | LIMIT 20

queries.name: Cell Tower Handoff Cascade Failure Analysis
queries.pain_point: Radio equipment failure and cell site power failures affecting handoffs, causing dropped calls during mobility
queries.description: Identify systematic cell tower handoff failures using multi-dimensional cardinality analysis, detect infrastructure cascade failures from power or equipment issues affecting 50+ cells, enriched with tower metadata
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_failure_events | WHERE handover_attempt == true AND failure_code IN ("HANDOVER_FAILURE", "TARGET_CELL_UNAVAILABLE", "RADIO_LINK_FAILURE") | LOOKUP JOIN cell_towers ON cell_id | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(5 minutes, @timestamp) | STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_cells = COUNT_DISTINCT(cell_id), unique_source_cells = COUNT_DISTINCT(source_cell_id), unique_target_cells = COUNT_DISTINCT(target_cell_id), unique_markets = COUNT_DISTINCT(cell_towers.market_name) BY time_bucket, cell_towers.power_source | INLINESTATS avg_cells = AVG(unique_cells), stddev_cells = STD_DEV(unique_cells), p95_cells = PERCENTILE(unique_cells, 95), avg_failures = AVG(failure_count) BY cell_towers.power_source | EVAL z_score_cells = (unique_cells - avg_cells) / COALESCE(stddev_cells, 1), cell_change_rate = unique_cells - avg_cells, pct_above_baseline = ((unique_cells - avg_cells) / avg_cells) * 100 | WHERE (z_score_cells > 2.5 OR unique_cells > 50) AND failure_count > 200 | EVAL severity = CASE(unique_cells > 100 AND z_score_cells > 3, "CRITICAL", unique_cells > 50 AND z_score_cells > 2.5, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP time_bucket, severity, unique_cells, failure_count, z_score_cells, unique_markets, cell_towers.power_source | SORT z_score_cells DESC | LIMIT 20

queries.name: MME Split-Brain and Signaling Storm Detection
queries.pain_point: Core network split-brain and signaling storms causing cyclical failures across multiple MME hosts
queries.description: Detect split-brain conditions and signaling storms by analyzing failure patterns across multiple MME hosts in same cluster, using statistical deviation to identify synchronized cyclical failures indicating core network issues
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_failure_events | WHERE failure_code IN ("SIGNALING_TIMEOUT", "OVERLOAD", "RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE", "S1_SETUP_FAILURE") | LOOKUP JOIN mme_hosts ON mme_host | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(5 minutes, @timestamp) | STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_mme_hosts = COUNT_DISTINCT(mme_host), unique_message_types = COUNT_DISTINCT(signaling_message_type), affected_subscribers = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi) BY time_bucket, mme_hosts.cluster_id, mme_hosts.datacenter | INLINESTATS avg_failures = AVG(failure_count), stddev_failures = STD_DEV(failure_count), avg_mme_hosts = AVG(unique_mme_hosts), p95_failures = PERCENTILE(failure_count, 95) BY mme_hosts.cluster_id | EVAL z_score_failures = (failure_count - avg_failures) / COALESCE(stddev_failures, 1), multi_host_indicator = CASE(unique_mme_hosts >= 2, 1, 0), storm_probability = z_score_failures * multi_host_indicator | WHERE z_score_failures > 3 AND unique_mme_hosts >= 2 AND failure_count > 200 | EVAL severity = CASE(unique_mme_hosts >= 3 AND z_score_failures > 4, "CRITICAL", unique_mme_hosts >= 2 AND z_score_failures > 3, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP time_bucket, severity, unique_mme_hosts, failure_count, affected_subscribers, mme_hosts.cluster_id, mme_hosts.datacenter | SORT storm_probability DESC | LIMIT 20

queries.name: Enterprise SLA Violation Risk Prediction
queries.pain_point: SLA violations with enterprise customers due to undetected service degradation
queries.description: Proactively identify service degradation affecting enterprise customers by analyzing failure patterns for enterprise subscribers, calculate SLA risk scores before violations occur and help desk is overwhelmed
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_failure_events | LOOKUP JOIN subscriber_profiles ON imsi | WHERE subscriber_profiles.enterprise_account == true | LOOKUP JOIN mme_hosts ON mme_host | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(5 minutes, @timestamp) | STATS enterprise_failures = COUNT(*), unique_enterprise_imsi = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi), unique_procedures = COUNT_DISTINCT(procedure_type), failure_rate = COUNT(*) BY time_bucket, mme_hosts.region, subscriber_profiles.plan_type | INLINESTATS avg_failures = AVG(enterprise_failures), stddev_failures = STD_DEV(enterprise_failures), p95_failures = PERCENTILE(enterprise_failures, 95), avg_imsi = AVG(unique_enterprise_imsi) BY mme_hosts.region | EVAL z_score = (enterprise_failures - avg_failures) / COALESCE(stddev_failures, 1), imsi_deviation = unique_enterprise_imsi - avg_imsi, sla_risk_score = (z_score * 0.6) + ((imsi_deviation / 100) * 0.4) | WHERE z_score > 2.5 AND enterprise_failures > 50 | EVAL severity = CASE(sla_risk_score > 4 AND unique_enterprise_imsi > 100, "CRITICAL", sla_risk_score > 3 AND unique_enterprise_imsi > 50, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP time_bucket, severity, unique_enterprise_imsi, enterprise_failures, sla_risk_score, mme_hosts.region, subscriber_profiles.plan_type | SORT sla_risk_score DESC | LIMIT 20

queries.name: Rogue Network and SS7 Security Attack Detection
queries.pain_point: Rogue network attempts and SS7 security attacks requiring immediate detection and response
queries.description: Detect security attacks and rogue network attempts by identifying unusual PLMN patterns, unauthorized roaming attempts, and suspicious failure signatures using semantic search for known attack patterns
queries.esql_query: FROM mme_failure_events | WHERE failure_code IN ("PLMN_NOT_ALLOWED", "ROAMING_NOT_ALLOWED", "ILLEGAL_UE", "IMSI_UNKNOWN_IN_HSS") OR SEMANTIC(failure_description, "spoofing attack SS7 vulnerability unauthorized access location tracking") | LOOKUP JOIN subscriber_profiles ON imsi | EVAL time_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(5 minutes, @timestamp), plmn_mismatch = CASE(serving_plmn != subscriber_profiles.home_plmn, 1, 0) | STATS attack_events = COUNT(*), unique_imsi = COUNT_DISTINCT(imsi), unique_plmn = COUNT_DISTINCT(serving_plmn), unique_tracking_areas = COUNT_DISTINCT(tracking_area_code), plmn_mismatches = SUM(plmn_mismatch) BY time_bucket, serving_plmn | INLINESTATS avg_events = AVG(attack_events), stddev_events = STD_DEV(attack_events), avg_plmn = AVG(unique_plmn) BY serving_plmn | EVAL z_score = (attack_events - avg_events) / COALESCE(stddev_events, 1), attack_diversity = unique_plmn + unique_tracking_areas, threat_score = (z_score * 0.5) + (attack_diversity * 0.3) + (plmn_mismatches * 0.2) | WHERE z_score > 3 OR (unique_imsi > 100 AND plmn_mismatches > 50) | EVAL severity = CASE(threat_score > 10 AND unique_imsi > 200, "CRITICAL", threat_score > 7 AND unique_imsi > 100, "HIGH", "MEDIUM") | KEEP time_bucket, severity, unique_imsi, attack_events, threat_score, serving_plmn, unique_plmn | SORT threat_score DESC | LIMIT 20
